ai-and-ml.name: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
ai-and-ml.tagline: Competent, socially and ethically responsible professionals in AI & Machine Learning through education, research and innovation.
ai-and-ml.overview: The department stands tall in both infrastructure and qualified, committed faculty. Housed in a picturesque building with a seminar hall, department library, faculty rooms and four computer laboratories equipped with 150+ state-of-the-art systems, it offers a pervasive, high-quality wired and Wi-Fi network and a wide range of application software (from databases and DTP to 3D animation and multimedia tools). The faculty pursue research in areas including Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Vehicular Ad-hoc Networks (VANET), Image Processing, Computer Networks, Mobile Computing, Natural Language Processing and Pattern Recognition, and have published books and papers in refereed journals and reputed conferences. The department has attracted more than ₹1.11 crore in sponsored infrastructure and research funding, and regularly conducts faculty, student and other development programmes.
ai-and-ml.vision: To develop competent, social and ethically responsible professionals in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning through excellence in education, research and innovation.
mission: Develop globally competent AIML professionals to address real-world problems.
Imbibe moral and socio-ethical values.
Cultivate a multidisciplinary environment that encourages collaboration and lifelong learning.
Foster a culture of research, innovation and entrepreneurship.

psos.text: Apply the fundamentals of mathematics, science and engineering to understand, analyze and develop AI-based complex systems.
Exhibit proficiency in artificial intelligence and machine learning to provide solutions to real-world problems across society.
Demonstrate the ability to lead and work toward an innovative career path and engage in lifelong learning with moral values.

highlights: Four computer laboratories equipped with 150+ state-of-the-art systems and high-speed wired/Wi-Fi networking.
Research strengths in AI, Machine Learning, VANET, Image Processing, NLP and Pattern Recognition.
More than ₹1.11 crore received in sponsored infrastructure and research funding.
Regular faculty, student and skill-development programmes in Information Technology.

automobile-engg.name: Automobile Engineering
automobile-engg.tagline: Academic excellence in automobile engineering through quality education and a research mindset for a sustainable future.
automobile-engg.overview: BVV Sangha's Basaveshwar Engineering College re-established the Automobile Engineering department in 2024-25 to develop automotive-specialized professionals in North Karnataka. With a sanctioned intake of 30 students and a carpet area of 11,000 sq. ft., the department houses dedicated laboratories — Automotive CAD, Automobile Servicing, Automobile Reconditioning, Automobile Scanning, Automotive Electrical & Electronics, and Vehicle Testing. With faculty averaging over 25 years of teaching experience and Ph.D.s across diverse research areas, the department has more than 50 national/international publications and well-qualified technical staff. It plans student SAE activities such as SUPRA, BAJA, EFFICYCLE and GO-KART, and has initiated revenue-generation activities including a motor-vehicle driving school and third-party inspection.
automobile-engg.vision: To achieve academic excellence in the realm of automobile engineering by fostering quality education and a research mindset for a sustainable future.
mission: Develop outstanding professionals in automobile engineering for future needs.

biotechnology.name: Biotechnology
biotechnology.tagline: A centre for academic excellence in biotechnology and innovative research for a sustainable future.
biotechnology.overview: The Department of Biotechnology was established in 2002 and is engaged in effective teaching-learning, basic research and technology-based training and service to society. It offers a 4-year B.E. in Biotechnology (intake 30) and is supported by well-equipped independent laboratories, classrooms, a seminar hall and library. The department has been recognised as an R&D (Ph.D.) Research Centre by VTU Belagavi since 2011, with seven faculty holding Ph.D. degrees and active research in food processing, biofuels technology, bioremediation, fermentation technology and molecular biology. It hosts a Bioenergy Research, Information and Demonstration Centre (BRIDC) in association with KSBDB, GoK and a Food Testing Laboratory supported by KITS, Government of Karnataka, and partners with industry to provide students opportunities for internships and projects.
biotechnology.vision: To be a centre for academic excellence in biotechnology and innovative research for a sustainable future.
mission: Develop technically competent and ethically oriented professionals.
Promote collaborations with academia, industries and research institutes for societal needs.
Strengthen the culture of research, innovation, entrepreneurship and environmental consciousness.

peos.text: Graduates will apply fundamental knowledge of biological sciences and engineering principles to solve problems in biotechnology and allied fields.
Graduates will excel and succeed in industry, research and technical professions through global and rigorous education.
Graduates will exhibit good scientific and engineering logic to comprehend, analyze, design and create novel products as well as solutions for real-time problems.
Graduates will exhibit professionalism, ethical attitude, oral and written communication skills, teamwork, lifelong learning and ethical values for a successful professional career.

psos.text: Graduates will be able to develop competency in applying engineering principles to environment, agriculture and healthcare systems.
Graduates will be able to design, optimize and manage bioprocesses for the development of bio-based products, ensuring quality, safety and regulatory compliance in the industries.

highlights: Established in 2002 with a B.E. intake of 30; NBA-accredited UG programme.
Recognised as a VTU R&D (Ph.D.) Research Centre since 2011, with seven Ph.D.-holding faculty and fourteen Ph.D.s awarded.
Hosts a Bioenergy Research, Information and Demonstration Centre (BRIDC) in association with KSBDB, Government of Karnataka.
Operates a Food Testing Laboratory and a poly-house with raceway ponds, supporting start-ups, patents and product development.

Research focus areas: Biochemical technology, Microbial Technology, Food processing, Biofuels technology, Solid waste management and Bioremediation, Fermentation technology, and Molecular biology and Bioinformatics.

Regular interaction with faculty from IITs, personnel from industry helped in enhancement and upgradation of the students' knowledge in the areas of Biotechnology. Regular visits and industrial internship to biotechnology, food and pharmaceutical companies helped students and faculty to keep themselves updated with technological changes.

associations.about: The department's student association, established in 2007, through which students organise extracurricular and co-curricular activities, participate in technical workshops, seminars and conferences, and conduct an annual intra-college technical fest.

biotechnology.about:
The Department of Biotechnology was established at Basaveshwar Engineering College in 2002, focusing on impactful teaching, research, and community service. The Department offers various programs including UG, PG Diploma, PG, and Ph.D., and has been recognized as an R&D center by VTU Belagavi since 2011.

The field of biotechnology stands at the intersection of engineering and technological advancement, encompassing diverse domains such as biopharmaceutical development, industrial applications, and environmental solutions. Central to this discipline is the application of genetic technologies across animal and plant biotechnology, as well as innovations in cell and tissue engineering. The COVID-19 pandemic underscored the critical role of biotechnology, with bio-pharmaceutical companies racing to develop vaccines and therapeutics, illuminating the industry's impact on public health. As of 2023, India emerges as a significant player in the global biotechnology landscape, ranking among the top twelve destinations for biotech innovation and holding the position as the largest biotechnology hub in the Asia Pacific region. The Indian biotechnology sector is valued at over USD 92 billion, with the bio-pharmaceutical industry leading in biosimilars, having the highest number approved in the domestic market. Moreover, the global bio-pharmaceutical market is projected to see remarkable growth, expanding from USD 274.10 billion in 2020 to an anticipated USD 703.28 billion by 2027, indicating a growing recognition of biotechnology's pivotal role in addressing contemporary challenges in healthcare and the environment.

civil-engg.name: Civil Engineering
civil-engg.tagline: A centre of excellence in teaching, research and innovation, shaping globally competent civil engineers for a sustainable society.
civil-engg.overview: The Civil Engineering Department is one of the pioneering departments since the inception of the college in 1963. It is engaged in imparting quality education and producing outstanding graduates through qualified faculty and state-of-the-art infrastructure. The department offers a 4-year B.E. (intake 120) and 2-year M.Tech. programme in Structural Engineering with intake 18. It has been recognised as a Research Centre by VTU since 2003 and as a QIP Research Centre (Ph.D.) by AICTE, New Delhi since 2012, and is a State Resource Centre for training in Earthquake Engineering under NPCBEERM. The department has organised conferences, seminars and workshops, fetched research grants worth ₹183 lakhs, and provides consultancy services for various government and private agencies.
civil-engg.vision: To be a centre of excellence in teaching, research and innovation, shaping globally competent civil engineers toward the sustainable development of society.
mission: Provide a student-centric learning environment, cultivating critical thinking and problem-solving skills in aspiring civil engineers.

Gain expertise in water management and environmental engineering to create sustainable solutions for water systems and environmental challenges.

highlights: A pioneering department since the college's inception in 1963, with B.E. intake grown from 30 to 120 students.

computer-science-and-engg.overview: The Department of Computer Science and Engineering was established in 1983. It offers an undergraduate programme (B.E.) in Computer Science and Engineering with an intake of 180 students, along with M.Sc. (Engineering by Research) and Ph.D. programmes. The department has highly qualified and well-experienced faculty committed to imparting quality technical education in IT and research. Its primary objectives include developing innovative skills, enriching academics through quality teaching-learning processes, building student confidence and inculcating entrepreneurial abilities — producing professionals with ethical values and societal concern.
computer-science-and-engg.vision: To be a Centre of Excellence in IT education, research and innovation, crafting competent professionals catering to the needs of society with moral and ethical values.
mission: To produce competent IT professionals committed to lifelong learning.
To promote interdisciplinary research for addressing societal needs.
To inculcate leadership qualities and entrepreneurial abilities with moral and ethical values.
To instill environmental consciousness through teaching-learning processes.

peos.text: Provide a strong educational foundation in Mathematics, Science, Basic Engineering and Computer Science.
Develop proficiency with an ability to solve a wide range of computational problems in industry, government or other work environments.
Inculcate professionalism by infusing leadership qualities, team spirit, effective communication skills, high ethical values and empathy for the needs of society.
Encourage students towards higher studies, research activities and entrepreneurial skills for lifelong learning.

psos.text: Inculcate analytical, logical, programming and managerial abilities.
Apply Software Engineering principles and practices to provide software solutions.
Design and develop computational systems for real-life applications.

electronics-and-communication-engg.name: Electronics & Communication Engineering
electronics-and-communication-engg.tagline: Excellence in electronics & communication engineering through quality education and research.
electronics-and-communication-engg.overview: The Department of Electronics and Communication (E&C) Engineering was established in 1985. It offers an undergraduate programme in Electronics and Communication Engineering along with Ph.D. programmes in cutting-edge areas such as VLSI Design, Digital Signal Processing, Wireless Networking, MEMS and Speech Signal Processing. Recognised as a Quality Improvement Programme (QIP) centre by the Government of India, the department has a strong legacy in research and development — particularly in communications, signal processing, microelectronics and RF/photonics. It hosts well-equipped laboratories spanning digital electronics, advanced digital communication, microwave communication, microprocessors and microcontrollers, and computer networks, along with an exclusive computing facility running industry-standard tools such as CADENCE VLSI, ECAD and VHDL simulation/synthesis packages. The department regularly organises seminars, workshops and training programmes under initiatives like TEQIP, ACME and AICTE, and has executed several AICTE-sponsored projects to strengthen its laboratory and research capabilities.
electronics-and-communication-engg.vision: To achieve excellence in electronics and communication engineering through quality education and research for developing competent professionals.
mission: Foster a dynamic teaching and learning process.
Encourage research through innovation and collaboration.
Imbibe moral, ethical values and social responsibilities.

associations.name: ACME — Association for Communication and Modern Electronics
associations.about: The Association for Communication and Modern Electronics (ACME) is the official student association of the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering. It serves as a dynamic platform for students to explore, engage, and excel beyond academics. ACME organizes a wide variety of events throughout the year, including technical workshops, seminars, sports competitions, cultural programs, fun activities, and celebrations of important days. The association aims to foster all-round development by encouraging creativity, teamwork, leadership, and technical excellence among students. By bridging academics with extracurricular involvement, ACME plays a vital role in enriching the campus experience for ECE students.

documents.title: Innovations in Teaching and Learning (Best Practices)

pos.text: Engineering knowledge: Apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems.

Problem analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences.

Design/development of solutions: Design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations.

Conduct investigations of complex problems: Use research-based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions.

Modern tool usage: Create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations.

The engineer and society: Apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice.

Environment and sustainability: Understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development.
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice.
Individual and team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multidisciplinary settings.

Communication: Communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as, being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions.

Project management and finance: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.
Life-long learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

electronics-and-communication-engg.about: Welcome to ECE at BEC. Since 1985, we've evolved from teaching basic circuits to advancing work in VLSI design, speech processing, wireless networks, MEMS, and emerging areas of electronics. Our students learn by building, experimenting, and questioning—because engineering is best understood through practice. We expect commitment, curiosity, and initiative, and in return, our faculty provide the guidance and support needed to grow. This department is yours to build—and we'll hold you to that trust.

electrical-and-electronics-engg.name: Electrical & Electronics Engineering
electrical-and-electronics-engg.tagline: Academic excellence, research and innovation in electrical & electronics engineering to meet energy, industrial and societal needs.
electrical-and-electronics-engg.overview: The Electrical and Electronics Engineering department was started with the inception of the college in 1963 and offers undergraduate, postgraduate and research programmes. It has well-equipped laboratories with state-of-the-art equipment and dedicated, qualified staff. The department has specialized faculty in Power Systems, Machines, Drives and Power Electronics, Renewable Energy Sources, Instrumentation, Signal Processing, HVDC and FACTS, and Power & Energy Systems. It is accredited by NBA and is a recognised Research Centre of VTU, Belagavi, offering a PG in Power & Energy Systems, Ph.D. and M.Sc. (Engg.) by research. Over the years the department has received 150+ lakhs in R&D grants and produced 16+ Ph.D.s, 75+ M.Techs and 90+ technical publications. Two new research labs in Renewable Energy and SCADA were established with support from VGST (Govt. of Karnataka) and World Bank TEQIP-II grants, and the department has MoUs with seven organisations.
electrical-and-electronics-engg.vision: To be in the global forefront of education, research, and innovation in Electrical and Electronics Engineering to meet the energy, ecology, industrial and societal needs.
mission: Practice dynamic teaching-learning processes adapting to ongoing global technological developments in electrical and electronics engineering.
Engage in national/international industry and institute collaborations for higher studies, research, development and innovation.
Carry out inter-disciplinary projects, skill-development activities and field visits to imbibe real-life experiences in students.
Render empathetic services to resolve energy, ecology and environmental issues.

pos.text: Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop solutions to complex engineering problems.

Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development. (WK1 to WK4)

Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required. (WK5)

Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis and interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions. (WK8)

Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modelling, recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems. (WK2 and WK6)

The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for their impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment. (WK1, WK5 and WK7)
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national and international laws. (WK9)
Individual and Collaborative Team Work: Function effectively as an individual and as a member or leader in diverse and multi-disciplinary teams.

Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, and make effective presentations considering cultural, language and learning differences.

Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one's own work, as a member and leader in a team, to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.

Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for and have the preparation and ability for (i) independent and life-long learning, (ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and (iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change. (WK8)

wk.text: A systematic, theory-based understanding of the natural sciences applicable to the discipline and awareness of relevant social sciences.

Conceptually-based mathematics, numerical analysis, data analysis, statistics and formal aspects of computer and information science to support detailed analysis and modelling applicable to the discipline.
A systematic, theory-based formulation of engineering fundamentals required in the engineering discipline.

Engineering specialist knowledge that provides theoretical frameworks and bodies of knowledge for the accepted practice areas in the engineering discipline; much is at the forefront of the discipline.

Knowledge, including efficient resource use, environmental impacts, whole-life cost, reuse of resources, net zero carbon and similar concepts, that supports engineering design and operations in a practice area.
Knowledge of engineering practice (technology) in the practice areas in the engineering discipline.

Knowledge of the role of engineering in society and identified issues in engineering practice in the discipline, such as the professional responsibility of an engineer to public safety and sustainable development.
Engagement with selected knowledge in the current research literature of the discipline, and awareness of the power of critical thinking and creative approaches to evaluate emerging issues.

Ethics, inclusive behaviour and conduct. Knowledge of professional ethics, responsibilities and norms of engineering practice. Awareness of the need for diversity by reason of ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability etc. with mutual understanding and respect, and of inclusive attitudes.

electronics-and-computer-engg.name: Electronics and Computer Engineering
electronics-and-computer-engg.tagline: An interdisciplinary department blending electronics engineering and computer science to design, develop and integrate innovative technologies.
electronics-and-computer-engg.overview: Electronics and Computer Engineering blends electronic engineering with computer science to design, develop and integrate innovative technologies across hardware, software and IT. Established in the academic year 2024-25, the department offers a 4-year B.E. programme with an intake of 60 students, taught by highly qualified faculty whose average teaching experience is 27 years. It is housed in a spacious new building with well-equipped IoT and computer laboratories of more than 60 computers, faculty rooms and a department office, all served by pervasive high-quality wired and Wi-Fi networks. The department aims to impart engineering education that meets the needs of society, industry and research, developing professionals with ethical values and societal concern who are equipped for both electronics/electrical industries and software companies.
electronics-and-computer-engg.vision: To be recognized as a Center of Excellence for Electronics and Computer Engineering education and research, developing competent professionals with moral and ethical values.
mission: Develop professionals with strong fundamentals, technical skills, committed to life-long learning.
Create an environment for interdisciplinary research by collaborating with industries and institutes of higher learning.
Imbibe high moral and ethical values.

peos.text: Apply knowledge of electronics and computing to design and develop intelligent products or systems, including IoT, robotics, and AI-based applications, addressing societal and industrial needs.
Develop expertise in designing, analyzing, and implementing electronic circuits, embedded systems, and signal processing applications to meet the needs of real-world challenges.
Impart proficiency in programming languages, software development, and modern computational tools to create innovative solutions for complex engineering problems.
Adapt to emerging technologies in electronics and computer engineering by engaging in continuous learning and contributing to sustainable and ethical technological advancements.

psos.text: Demonstrate the ability to design, develop a product/system by integrating concepts from electronics and computer engineering to solve real-world problems.

Inculcate the expertise in modern technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, IoT, and Cyber-Physical Systems, and apply them to create innovative and sustainable engineering solutions.

highlights: Established in the academic year 2024-25, the department offers a B.E. program with an approved intake of 60 students.
The department is housed in a spacious modern building equipped with state-of-the-art VLSI, FPGA, Embedded Systems, IoT, and Computer laboratories featuring over 60 computers.
The program prepares graduates to pursue diverse career opportunities in software industries, electronics and electrical sectors.
Highly qualified faculty with an average teaching experience of 27 years.
Pervasive, high-quality wired and Wi-Fi networking across the department.

industrial-and-production-engg.name: Industrial & Production Engineering
industrial-and-production-engg.tagline: A centre of excellence in Industrial and Production Engineering education, research and practice, shaping globally competent leaders for manufacturing and service industries.
industrial-and-production-engg.overview: The Department of Industrial & Production Engineering was established in 1984 with an initial intake of 30 students. It prepares leaders capable of managing manufacturing and service-industry operations across the two domains of Industrial Engineering and Production Engineering. Students gain exposure to modern manufacturing technologies including CAD/CAM, CAE, FMS, CIM and additive manufacturing, alongside management concepts taught through practical case studies, supported by fully doctorate-qualified faculty, well-equipped laboratories and experienced technical staff. The department has been a recognised research centre since 2008, with 13 candidates awarded Ph.D. to date.
industrial-and-production-engg.vision: To achieve excellence in education and research in Industrial and Production Engineering for a sustainable future.
mission: Develop globally competent professionals in Industrial and Production Engineering by imparting quality education.
Nurture a culture of research, innovation, and entrepreneurship through collaboration with institutes and industries.
Imbibe moral, ethical, environmental, and societal consciousness.
programsOffered: M.Sc. (Engineering) by Research — Industrial & Production Engineering

peos.text: To prepare graduates for successful careers in manufacturing, service, R&D, academic, and allied engineering professions by providing strong fundamentals and the ability to meet global industry challenges.

To develop professional competence in design, manufacturing, maintenance, quality, and computational aspects of products and services, enabling graduates to contribute effectively as industrial engineers and managers.
To nurture lifelong learning, ethical practice, creativity, communication, and entrepreneurial ability, with a strong multidisciplinary outlook for continuous professional growth.

psos.text: IPE graduates will competently design, implement, enhance and oversee systems and activities within the manufacturing and service sectors.
Graduates apply industrial engineering expertise to construct mathematical models and examine practical organizational challenges, producing viable interventions.
Graduates demonstrate professional competency in industrial environments or entrepreneurial ventures by integrating manufacturing and management methodologies.

highlights: Established in 1984; among the few dedicated Industrial & Production Engineering programmes in the region.
Recognised research centre since 2008 with major research areas in manufacturing, design, management, industrial engineering and materials science; 13 Ph.D.s awarded to date.
Fully doctorate-qualified faculty with expertise spanning CAD/CAM, CAE, FMS, CIM and additive manufacturing.

Funded projects from AICTE and TEQIP including Modelling and Optimization of Additive Manufacturing (₹34.5 lakhs, TEQIP) and Management Science & Advanced Machining Science Laboratories (₹28.76 lakhs, TEQIP).

information-science-and-engg.name: Information Science & Engineering
information-science-and-engg.tagline: A global premier centre of education and research in Information Science and Engineering.
information-science-and-engg.overview: The Department of Information Science and Engineering was established in 1999 and offers a 4-year B.E. programme in Information Science and Engineering with a sanctioned intake of 180. Backed by qualified faculty and well-equipped computing laboratories, the department trains students in information and communication technologies, software engineering and emerging computing domains. It runs a recognised Research Centre (established 2020) with active research in image processing and pattern recognition, wireless sensor and ubiquitous networks, vehicular ad-hoc networks, IoT and cloud computing, and data mining and machine/deep learning. The B.E. programme has been accredited by the NBA, and the department maintains strong industry linkages, internships and a vibrant student association.
information-science-and-engg.vision: To be a Global Premier Center of Education and Research in Information Science and Engineering.
mission: To impart quality engineering education developing ethical and competent professionals.
To carry out interdisciplinary research to fulfil the needs of various sections of society, including government and private organizations.
To create exemplary young professionals who will promote sustainable development.

peos.text: To provide students with a strong foundation in mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals.
To provide exposure to emerging cutting-edge information and communication technologies for achieving excellence in the global Information Technology industry.
To equip students with the spirit of innovation to carry out research-type projects to fulfil the changing needs of society.
To inculcate teamwork, leadership qualities, and entrepreneurial and communication skills among students.
To motivate and nurture students to pursue higher education, engage in lifelong learning, and be socially responsible and morally upright Information Technology professionals.

psos.text: Apply programming paradigms, modern tools, and ethical practices to develop sustainable Software Systems.
Adopt computing and communication models along with data engineering techniques to develop secured intelligent Information Systems.

highlights: Established in 1999, offering a 4-year B.E. programme in Information Science and Engineering with an intake of 180.
NBA-accredited B.E. programme under the autonomous, VTU-affiliated institute.
VTU-recognised Research Centre (established 2020) across image processing, wireless sensor and vehicular networks, IoT and cloud computing, and machine/deep learning.
Active industry linkages and internships, with the student association RISE conducting webinars, training programmes and technical events.

mechanical-engg.name: Mechanical Engineering
mechanical-engg.tagline: Excelling in mechanical engineering education, research and innovation for sustainable development.
mechanical-engg.overview: Established in 1963, the Department of Mechanical Engineering is one of the oldest and largest departments of Basaveshwar Engineering College, Bagalkote. The department offers a B.E. programme with an annual intake of 120 students and has a recognized Research and Development Centre offering M.Sc. (Engg.) and Ph.D. programmes under VTU, Belagavi. It is also a recognized QIP Centre of AICTE, Government of India. The B.E. programme is NBA-accredited under the Tier-I framework aligned with the Washington Accord up to June 2028. Faculty members have successfully completed sponsored research projects worth over Rs. 300 lakhs, published over 500 research papers along with books and book chapters and guided 66 research scholars to Ph.D. degrees. Supported by Government funding agencies such as DRDO, DST, CSIR, NRB and VGST, the department provides excellent research facilities, strong industry interaction, industrial exposure and career opportunities in both core and IT sectors.
mechanical-engg.vision: To excel in Mechanical Engineering education, research and innovation for sustainable development.
mission: Develop students with the necessary knowledge and skills to meet societal needs.
Enable students to adopt changing technological advancements for employment and entrepreneurship.
Conduct research to develop intellectual property.
Strengthen the linkage between industry and academia.

peos.text: The Mechanical Engineering students should be able to engage in the design and to develop the components and equipments in the field of mechanical engineering industries and other allied industries.

The graduates should be able to apply the knowledge of mechanical engineering to solve problems of social relevance and pursue higher education studies and research in the field of materials science, machine design, manufacturing and thermal/fluid engineering.
The graduates will work effectively as individuals and as team members in interdisciplinary projects.
The graduates should be able to engage in lifelong learning, to become an entrepreneur, apply ethics and adapt to changing professional and societal requirements.

psos.text: Apply core concepts of mechanical engineering to design, analyze, and solve mechanical systems and components by utilizing modern tools, computational techniques, and engineering practices in multidisciplinary environments.

Identify, formulate, and implement solutions to real-world mechanical engineering problems by integrating knowledge from multidisciplinary domains, including IoT and AI, while upholding ethical standards and effective communication within diverse teams.

highlights: Established in 1963, one of the oldest and largest departments of the institute.
Offers B.E. in Mechanical Engineering (Intake: 120) under VTU, Belagavi.
Recognized Research and Development Centre since 2000, offering M.Sc. (Engg.) and Ph.D. programmes under VTU, Belagavi.
Recognized as a QIP Centre by AICTE, Government of India.
Total student strength of around 500, including UG and research scholars.
The B.E. programme has been accredited by NBA under the Tier-I framework aligned with the Washington Accord from 2003–2006, 2006–2009, 2016–2019, 2019–2022, 2022–2025, and 2025–June 2028.
Highly qualified and experienced faculty with strong academic and research credentials.
Sponsored research projects worth over Rs. 300 lakhs and over 500 research publications, books and book chapters.
66 research scholars have successfully completed their Ph.D. degrees.

Research and infrastructure supported by Government funding agencies like DRDO and NRB (Ministry of Defence, Government of India), DST and CSIR (Ministry of Science and Technology, Government of India), Board of Research in Fusion Science & Technology (BRFST, National Fusion Programme, Institute of Plasma Research, Gandhinagar, Gujarat), AICTE (MHRD, Government of India) and VGST (Government of Karnataka).
Facilities for advanced research in material development, testing and consultancy.
Strong industry interaction through internships, industrial visits, expert lectures, and MoUs.

Student skill development is supported through MEA (Mechanical Engineering Association) and Centres of Excellence in Advanced Materials Testing, BEC-Bosch Rexroth Centre for Industrial Automation, Mechatronics and Robotics.
Excellent placement opportunities in both core engineering and IT sectors.
Curriculum aligned with Outcome-Based Education (OBE), industry-specific competencies, and NEP-2020 principles.
Strong sports culture with students representing the university and earning University Blues.

associations.name: Mechanical Engineering Association (MEA)
associations.about: Established in 1988-89, the MEA encourages and enhances the technical and other skills of students and conducts technical, cultural and sports activities through the year for the overall development of students, helping build personality, decision-making, organisational skills, teamwork and coordination.

wk.text: A systematic, theory-based understanding of the natural sciences applicable to the discipline and awareness of relevant social sciences.

Conceptually-based mathematics, numerical analysis, data analysis, statistics and formal aspects of computer and information science to support detailed analysis and modelling applicable to the discipline.
A systematic, theory-based formulation of engineering fundamentals required in the engineering discipline.

Engineering specialist knowledge that provides theoretical frameworks and bodies of knowledge for the accepted practice areas in the engineering discipline; much is at the forefront of the discipline.

Knowledge, including efficient resource use, environmental impacts, whole-life cost, re-use of resources, net zero carbon, and similar concepts, that supports engineering design and operations in a practice area.
Knowledge of engineering practice (technology) in the practice areas in the engineering discipline.

Knowledge of the role of engineering in society and identified issues in engineering practice in the discipline, such as the professional responsibility of an engineer to public safety and sustainable development.
Engagement with selected knowledge in the current research literature of the discipline, awareness of the power of critical thinking and creative approaches to evaluate emerging issues.

Ethics, inclusive behavior and conduct. Knowledge of professional ethics, responsibilities, and norms of engineering practice. Awareness of the need for diversity by reason of ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability etc. with mutual understanding and respect, and of inclusive attitudes.

pos.text: Engineering Knowledge: Apply knowledge of mathematics, natural science, computing, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization as specified in WK1 to WK4 respectively to develop to the solution of complex engineering problems.

Problem Analysis: Identify, formulate, review research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions with consideration for sustainable development (WK1 to WK4).

Design/Development of Solutions: Design creative solutions for complex engineering problems and design/develop systems/components/processes to meet identified needs with consideration for the public health and safety, whole-life cost, net zero carbon, culture, society and environment as required (WK5).

Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems: Conduct investigations of complex engineering problems using research-based knowledge including design of experiments, modelling, analysis & interpretation of data to provide valid conclusions (WK8).

Engineering Tool Usage: Create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources and modern engineering & IT tools, including prediction and modelling recognizing their limitations to solve complex engineering problems (WK2 and WK6).

The Engineer and The World: Analyze and evaluate societal and environmental aspects while solving complex engineering problems for its impact on sustainability with reference to economy, health, safety, legal framework, culture and environment (WK1, WK5, and WK7).
Ethics: Apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics, human values, diversity and inclusion; adhere to national & international laws (WK9).
Individual and Collaborative Team work: Function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse/multi-disciplinary teams.

Communication: Communicate effectively and inclusively within the engineering community and society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations considering cultural, language, and learning differences.

Project Management and Finance: Apply knowledge and understanding of engineering management principles and economic decision-making and apply these to one’s own work, as a member and leader in a team, and to manage projects and in multidisciplinary environments.

Life-Long Learning: Recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability for i) independent and life-long learning ii) adaptability to new and emerging technologies and iii) critical thinking in the broadest context of technological change (WK8).

physics.name: Physics
physics.tagline: A centre of excellence for education and research in physics.
physics.overview: The Physics department was started with the inception of the college in 1963 and has well-equipped laboratories. Students excel under the guidance of qualified and dedicated staff, and faculty and student development programmes are organised regularly. The department has been a recognised Research Centre of VTU, Belagavi since 2013, with research focused on material science and magnetoelectric composites. At present four faculty and four supporting staff work in the department, actively engaged in teaching undergraduate students and pursuing research.
physics.vision: To be recognized as a centre of excellence for education and research in the field of physics.
mission: Pursue excellence by imparting education in physics through a student-centric, dynamic teaching-learning process.

chemistry.name: Chemistry
chemistry.tagline: Foundational chemistry for engineering — teaching, research and innovation.
chemistry.overview:
The Department of Chemistry serves as a crucial stepping stone in shaping young minds for successful engineering careers. The courses offered during the first year provide a strong scientific and analytical foundation, enabling students to understand core engineering concepts and develop the problem-solving skills essential for their future studies.

The department offers courses that emphasize the application of basic sciences to engineering, fostering innovation, critical thinking, and a multidisciplinary approach to learning. These courses help students build a solid understanding of fundamental principles while preparing them to meet the challenges of modern engineering and technology.

The department is supported by highly qualified, experienced, and dedicated faculty members who strive to create an engaging and dynamic learning environment. Through effective teaching methodologies and continuous mentoring, faculty members inspire students to achieve academic excellence and adapt successfully to the engineering curriculum.

Beyond academics, students are encouraged to actively participate in various co-curricular and extracurricular activities that contribute to their overall personality development. The department places strong emphasis on practical learning, enabling students to acquire hands-on experience and develop the skills required for their professional growth.

We are committed to providing a healthy, supportive, and student-friendly atmosphere that nurtures confidence, creativity, leadership, and lifelong learning. The Department of Chemistry aims to equip students with the knowledge, skills, and values necessary to excel in their chosen engineering disciplines and become responsible professionals and citizens.
chemistry.about: The Department of Chemistry serves as a foundational pillar in engineering education by imparting essential scientific knowledge and fostering analytical and problem-solving skills among undergraduate students. The department is committed to providing quality education through an outcome-based teaching–learning process that aligns with the vision and mission of the institution and supports the holistic development of future engineers.
chemistry.vision: To be recognized as a premier technical institute committed to developing exemplary professionals, offering research-based innovative solutions and inspiring inventions for holistic socio-economic development.
mission: Pursue excellence through student-centric, dynamic teaching-learning processes, encouraging freedom of inquiry and openness to change.
Carry out innovative, cutting-edge research and transfer technology for industrial and societal needs.
Imbibe moral and ethical values and develop compassionate human professionals.
highlights: Delivers stream-wise Applied Chemistry courses tailored to the CSE, EEE, Civil and Mechanical engineering streams of the first-year curriculum.
Integrated theory-and-laboratory courses (3:0:2:0) covering energy systems, corrosion science, polymers, nanomaterials, sensors, display systems and e-waste management.
Active faculty research in biofuel/biodiesel production from non-edible oils and in synthesis and characterization of ferrite nanomaterials, with publications in international journals.
Well-equipped analytical chemistry laboratory with colorimeters, pH meters, potentiometers and conductometers supporting practical coursework.

Applied Chemistry for Civil Engineering stream : 1BCHC102C/202C

mathematics.name: Mathematics
mathematics.tagline: A foundational teaching-learning centre imparting mathematical knowledge and computational thinking across all engineering disciplines.
mathematics.overview: The Department of Mathematics, established in 1963, has emerged as a successful teaching-learning centre imparting mathematics knowledge across the engineering disciplines of the college. It develops computational skills, critical thinking and problem-solving abilities in engineering students through core and elective courses, with remedial instruction for students who need additional support. The department comprises highly experienced and dedicated faculty, many holding Ph.D. degrees from reputed universities and a few pursuing their doctoral research, who have published widely in national and international journals.
mathematics.vision: The Department of Mathematics offers courses of study that ensure the student learner is able to contribute to today's society.
mission: Assist students in acquiring a conceptual understanding of the nature of mathematics and its applications.
Develop computational skills, critical thinking and problem-solving abilities through the study of mathematics.
Enable students to critically assess numerical and graphical information and formulate strategies for solving problems.
programsOffered: B.E. — Engineering Mathematics (service courses across all UG programmes)
highlights: A foundational basic-sciences department serving every engineering programme since the college's inception in 1963.
Faculty include several Ph.D. holders from reputed universities, with active doctoral research in Graph Theory, Fluid Dynamics, MHD and Numerical Analysis.
Faculty have published widely in national and international journals.
Offers core mathematics courses, electives and remedial instruction for engineering students.

humanities.name: Humanities & Social Sciences
humanities.tagline: An interdisciplinary department shaping engineers into legally, linguistically, socially and ethically conscious individuals.
humanities.overview: The Department of Humanities and Social Sciences, started in 2006, is an interdisciplinary department of Basaveshwar Engineering College, Bagalkote, that seeks to make technologists legally, linguistically, socially and ethically conscious individuals. Over the years, various subjects of humanities and social sciences have been introduced to widen the intellectual horizon of technical students. Today, the department functions at the intersections of communication, languages, Constitution of India, Intellectual Property Rights, Soft Skills, Universal Human Values, Scientific Foundations of Health and Innovation and Design Thinking. It focuses on students' inner character so that they contribute to building a value-based society.
humanities.vision: To be unique and versatile unit of the institution, to promote the constitutional values with language proficiency, ethics, scientific temper and humanism, inculcating practices of good health and wellness, spirit of nationality among engineering graduates.
mission: Develop globally competent professionals for future talent requirements.
Sharpening practical skills by providing value-based education.
Imbibe moral and ethical values and foster ecological and environmental consciousness.
Develop multi-tasking talent in graduates.
highlights: Interdisciplinary department established in 2006, serving all engineering programmes of the college.

Offers core, mandatory and elective courses in Constitution of India, Intellectual Property Rights, Communicative English, Kannada (Samskruthika and Balake), Soft Skills, Universal Human Values, Scientific Foundations of Health, and Innovation and Design Thinking.
A well-established Language Laboratory supported by duly qualified faculty.
Best practices include in-classroom online quizzes after each topic and student seminars with PPT presentations to build communication skills and stage confidence.

pg/environmental-engg.name: M.Tech. in Environmental Engineering
pg/environmental-engg.tagline: A two-year postgraduate programme of the Department of Civil Engineering, focused on sustainable environmental and water-quality engineering.
pg/environmental-engg.overview: The Department of Civil Engineering started the M.Tech. in Environmental Engineering in 2004 with an intake of 18. Admissions are offered through PGCET/GATE and the Quality Improvement Programme (QIP). The programme is AICTE recognized, NAAC accredited and permanently affiliated to VTU, and is supported by a well-equipped Environmental Engineering laboratory with advanced analytical instrumentation, computational facilities, and a Mobile Water Testing Laboratory used for rural community outreach.
pg/environmental-engg.vision: To be a center of excellence of higher learning and research in civil engineering encompassing ethical, environmental and economical aspects of the society.
mission: Prepare globally competent engineers through dynamic teaching-learning and research processes.
Provide knowledge base and consultancy services to the community in Civil Engineering areas.

peos.text: Graduates of the Programme will be effective environmental engineers to serve in the various industry, academia and research organizations.
Graduates will be able to understand, learn and apply appropriate techniques of prediction and modeling with an understanding of implications in long term sustainability.

Graduates are able to learn and apply new developments and tools in solving complex real life environmental engineering problems, and carry out interdisciplinary research to provide efficient, sustainable and ethical solutions.

highlights: M.Tech. in Environmental Engineering started in 2004 with an intake of 18.
AICTE recognized, NAAC accredited and permanently affiliated to VTU.
Well-equipped Environmental Engineering laboratory with advanced analytical equipment.
Computational facilities with specialized software (ENVI, Design Expert).
Mobile Water Testing Laboratory for rural community outreach.
Faculty engaged in high-quality research and consultancy services.

pg/geo-technical-engg.name: M.Tech. in Geo-Technical Engineering
pg/geo-technical-engg.tagline: A two-year postgraduate programme of the Department of Civil Engineering, specialising in soil mechanics, foundation engineering and geotechnical research.
pg/geo-technical-engg.overview: The Department of Civil Engineering started the M.Tech. in Geo-Technical Engineering in 1994 with an intake of 18. The programme emphasises fundamental principles, creative problem-solving in geotechnical challenges, and the analytical abilities needed for interdisciplinary work. It is AICTE recognized, NAAC accredited and permanently affiliated to VTU, supported by independent classrooms with interactive boards and a state-of-the-art geotechnical laboratory with cyclic triaxial, digital static triaxial, plate load and consolidation testing equipment.
pg/geo-technical-engg.vision: To be a center of excellence of higher learning and research in civil engineering encompassing ethical, environmental and economical aspects of the society.
mission: Prepare globally competent engineers responsive to economic and technological growth through dynamic teaching-learning and research processes.
Provide knowledge base and consultancy services to the community in Civil Engineering areas.

peos.text: Graduates of the program will become effective Geotechnical Engineers in Government, industry, or other organizations, designing, improving and implementing efficient, sustainable Geotechnical engineering practices.

Graduates of the program will provide solutions to Geotechnical Engineering problems that account for economical, societal and ethical standards, both as individuals and in team environments, by applying acquired engineering knowledge.
Graduates of the program will continue their lifelong learning to remain effective professionals and to maintain and enhance technical and professional growth.

highlights: M.Tech. in Geo-Technical Engineering started in 1994 with an intake of 18.
AICTE recognized, NAAC accredited and permanently affiliated to VTU.
State-of-the-art laboratory with cyclic triaxial, digital static triaxial, plate load and consolidation testing equipment.
Independent classrooms with interactive boards.
Faculty engaged in high-quality research and consultancy activities.

pg/structural-engg.name: M.Tech. in Structural Engineering
pg/structural-engg.tagline: A two-year postgraduate programme of the Department of Civil Engineering, focused on analysis, design and research in structural engineering.
pg/structural-engg.overview: The Civil Engineering Department is one of the pioneering departments of the college, established at its inception in 1963. The M.Tech. in Structural Engineering, with an intake of 18, provides postgraduate education in structural analysis and design supported by advanced laboratory facilities and research recognition from VTU and AICTE. Admissions are offered through PGCET/GATE and the Quality Improvement Programme (QIP), and the curriculum emphasises fundamental principles and creative problem-solving.
pg/structural-engg.vision: To be a center of excellence of higher learning and research in civil engineering encompassing ethical, environmental and economical aspects of the society.
mission: Prepare globally competent engineers through dynamic teaching-learning and research processes.
Provide knowledge base and consultancy services to the community in Civil Engineering areas.

peos.text: Graduates of the programme will become effective Structural engineers in government, industry, or other organizations, designing, improving and implementing efficient, sustainable Structural engineering practices.

Graduates of the programme will provide solutions to Structural engineering problems that account for economical, societal and ethical standards, both as individuals and in team environments, by applying acquired engineering knowledge.
Graduates of the programme will continue their lifelong learning to remain effective professionals and to maintain and enhance technical and professional growth.

highlights: M.Tech. in Structural Engineering with an intake of 18.
AICTE recognized, NAAC accredited and VTU affiliated.
State-of-the-art Structural Engineering laboratory with advanced testing equipment (actuator, loading frames, compression machines).
Computational facilities with ANSYS, ETABS, STAAD and AutoCAD.
Faculty engaged in research and consultancy activities.

pg/electrical-and-electronics-engg.name: M.Tech. in Energy Science and Technology
pg/electrical-and-electronics-engg.tagline: Postgraduate study in energy systems, electrical engineering applications and research.
pg/electrical-and-electronics-engg.overview: The Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering was started with the inception of the college in 1963. The department offers graduate, postgraduate and research programmes through VTU and QIP. It has well-equipped laboratories, a separate computer centre with 25 P-IV IBM desktop PCs connected to the campus network, a seminar hall with modern audio-visual teaching aids, licensed software including MATLAB, Mi-Power and AutoCAD Electrical, and a department library with more than 1000 books in E&EE. The M.Tech. in Energy Science and Technology is an interdisciplinary programme covering solar, wind energy, biogas and biomass, electricity storage, electric vehicles, sustainability, reliability, O&M of SPV and wind plants, energy management, green buildings, smart grids, microgrids and IoT applications in energy systems.
pg/electrical-and-electronics-engg.vision: To be in the global forefront of academic excellence, research and innovation in Electrical and Electronics Engineering to influence and meet energy, environment, industrial and societal needs.
mission: Practice dynamic teaching-learning processes adapting to ongoing global technological developments in Electrical and Electronics Engineering.
Involve the department in national and international industry-institute collaborations for higher studies, research, development and innovation.
Carry out interdisciplinary activities that contribute to energy, environment, industrial and societal needs.

mca.name: Master of Computer Applications (MCA)
mca.tagline: Educate and empower students for the IT profession with techno-social values.
mca.overview: The MCA programme is a two-year course offered within the autonomous framework granted to the college by VTU and UGC in 2007. The department offers a student-friendly learning atmosphere, a syllabus framed in consultation with industry experts, a software development cell that lets students experience live projects, and state-of-the-art computing laboratories built on open-source technologies.
mca.vision: Educate and empower students for the IT profession with techno-social values.
mission: Impart quality IT education and encourage students to showcase the best of their abilities.
Strengthen technical skills and imbibe moral and ethical values beneficial to society.
Produce graduates who accomplish the best professional standards.
highlights: Part of the college's autonomous status granted by VTU and UGC in 2007.
Software development cell for live, industry-style projects.
State-of-the-art computing laboratories built on open-source technologies.
Syllabus framed in consultation with industry experts.

mba.name: Master of Business Administration (MBA)
mba.tagline: A centre of excellence in management education, research and entrepreneurship for a sustainable future.
mba.overview: The Department of Management Studies was established at Basaveshwar Engineering College in 2009 to impart quality management education. It offers a two-year full-time MBA programme with an intake of 60 students spread over four semesters. The programme is autonomous, affiliated to VTU, Belagavi and approved by AICTE, New Delhi. The department is equipped with the necessary infrastructure, and its faculty are drawn from different specializations with varied experience. Practical labs such as a Presentation Lab and a Business Analytics Lab give students hands-on exposure to the presentation, communication, technical and analytical skills required of today's and tomorrow's managers.
mba.about: The Department of Management Studies (MBA) at Basaveshwar Engineering College offers industry-focused management education through experienced faculty, a contemporary curriculum, and experiential learning practices. The department emphasizes industry interaction, skill development, research, innovation, and strong placement support, preparing students to excel in diverse managerial and entrepreneurial roles.
mba.vision: To be a centre of excellence in management education, research and entrepreneurship for a sustainable future.
mission: Provide quality education in management to meet future talent requirements.
Nurture a culture of research and entrepreneurship for a sustainable future.
Imbibe moral and ethical values.
programsOffered: MBA — Master of Business Administration (2-year, full-time)

pos.text: Apply knowledge of management theories and practices to solve business problems.
Foster analytical and critical thinking abilities for data based decision making.
Ability to develop value-based leadership.
Ability to understand, analyze and communicate global, economic, legal and ethical aspects of business.
Ability to lead themselves and others in the achievement of organizational goals contributing effectively to a team environment.

highlights: Established in 2009; an autonomous programme affiliated to VTU and approved by AICTE, with an intake of 60.
Specializations in Marketing, Finance, Human Resources, Operations and Business Analytics.
Dedicated Presentation Lab and Business Analytics Lab for practical exposure.
Guided by core values of ethics, integrity, empathy and mutual respect.
Management Club: where ideas transform into leadership and professional growth.
Industry engagement: Bridging academia and industry through interactions and experiential learning.

associations.name: BEMSA — BEC Management Students Association
associations.about: Established in 2009, BEMSA brings students onto a common platform to share ideas and experiences and to organise executive meets, student and personality development programmes, sports and cultural events, improving communication, managerial, presentation and teamwork skills.

peos.text: MBA is a two-year full-time programme, aimed at nurturing and training young minds with contemporary skills of management, adept in handling diverse sectors of the economy.

The programme intends to inculcate leadership qualities in individuals to strategically position themselves in all emerging platforms of idea generation, creation of pragmatic knowledge, skills and competency development.

The diverse course curriculum enables a high degree of academic flexibility for fostering innovation and creativity. It instills resilience and adaptability in students for facing the challenges of the contemporary business world.

psos.text: Strategic and Analytical Skills: Apply management knowledge, critical thinking, and analytical skills to solve complex business problems and drive innovation.

Leadership, Entrepreneurial and Ethical Responsibility: Demonstrate leadership, entrepreneurial skills, teamwork, and ethical decision-making to achieve organizational goals and address societal challenges.
Global and Technological Adaptability: Adapt to global business environments and leverage modern tools and technologies for effective communication and decision-making.
